name: GeoSPARQL Topological Query Functions: Simple Features

examples.title: Usage example of geof:sfEquals: SPARQL Query and sample data
examples.content: A geometry should be equal to itself. The SPARQL query tests this equality relationship using the function geof:sfEquals

snippets.code:
PREFIX geo: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>

ASK
WHERE {
    ?f geo:sfEquals ?f2 .
    ?f geo:hasGeometry ?fgeom . ?fgeom geo:asWKT ?fgwkt .
    ?f2 geo:hasGeometry ?f2geom . ?f2geom geo:asWKT ?f2gwkt .
    FILTER (geof:sfEquals(?fgwkt,?f2gwkt)=true)
}

examples.title: Usage example of geof:sDisjoint: SPARQL Query and sample data
examples.content: Two disjoint geometries. The SPARQL query tests their disjointness using the function geof:sfDisjoint

snippets.code:
PREFIX geo: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>

ASK
WHERE {
    ?f geo:sfDisjoint ?f2 .
    ?f geo:hasGeometry ?fgeom . ?fgeom geo:asWKT ?fgwkt .
    ?f2 geo:hasGeometry ?f2geom . ?f2geom geo:asWKT ?f2gwkt .
    FILTER (geof:sfDisjoint(?fgwkt,?f2gwkt)=true)
}
